name: Portillo Lunada path to Castro Valnera
name_original: Senda Portillo Lunada al Castro Valnera
region: National Park los Collados del Asón
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around National Park los Collados del Asón traverse a landscape shaped by ancient glacial activity and karstic processes in Cantabria, Spain. The region features dramatic limestone outcrops, diverse ecosystems including beech and oak forests, and meadows. Key natural features include the Asón River, the 70-meter Asón Waterfall, and the glacial Circo de Hondojón. This largely unpopulated area offers a tranquil environment for outdoor exploration. Best bus station hiking trails around National Park los Collados del Asón The most popular bus station hiking route is Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 972 feet (296 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Plaza de Las Machorras – Grazing area loop from Las Machorras, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path. This route offers varied terrain with an ascent of 1,205 feet (367 meters). Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from La Pedrosa, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around National Park los Collados del Asón is defined by its dramatic limestone formations, glacial cirques, and diverse forest ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in National Park los Collados del Asón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,800 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park los Collados del Asón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus-accessible hiking trails are available in National Park Los Collados del Asón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 bus-accessible hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; in National Park Los Collados del Asón. These routes offer a range of moderate to difficult options for exploring the park's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes accessible from bus stations?
A: Yes, several circular routes are accessible. For a moderate loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazing area – Plaza de Las Machorras loop from Las Machorras&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic grazing areas. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging circular hike, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Porracolina – Pico Porracolina loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on these bus-accessible hikes?
A: The bus-accessible hikes in National Park Los Collados del Asón showcase the region's dramatic karstic formations and glacial legacy. You'll encounter stunning limestone outcrops, diverse ecosystems including beech and oak groves, and the impressive Asón River. Keep an eye out for the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 70-meter cascade, and the awe-inspiring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4338843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Honda&lt;/a&gt;, a 700-meter-long canyon carved by glaciers.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes accessible by public transport?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes accessible by bus. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996940056/pico-porracolina-via-calseca-stream-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a significant ascent to a prominent peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Porracolina – Pico Porracolina loop from La Pedrosa&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are the best viewpoints along the bus-accessible hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight providing expansive views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4902310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brenarromán Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via several paths, offers wonderful vistas of the Cantabrian interior landscape. You can also find viewpoints like the Mirador de la Cascada del Asón to observe the stunning valleys and birds of prey.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in National Park Los Collados del Asón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.41 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from 229 ratings. Reviewers often praise the tranquil, unspoiled natural environment, the dramatic geological features, and the sense of peace found amidst the breathtaking scenery.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife on hikes from bus stations?
A: Yes, the diverse ecosystems of Collados del Asón support a wide array of Cantabrian mountain fauna. Hikers often spot griffon vultures and Egyptian vultures soaring overhead. The numerous caves in the area are also home to various bat species. Keep a keen eye out, especially in the more secluded parts of the trails.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks to look out for on these routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sía Pass&lt;/a&gt; offers historical and scenic interest. Don't miss the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4338843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Honda&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4902310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brenarromán&lt;/a&gt; plateau is also a significant point of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in National Park Los Collados del Asón from bus stations?
A: The park offers rewarding experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, making them ideal for hiking. Summer is also popular, though some routes can be exposed. Winter hiking is possible, but conditions can be challenging with snow and ice, especially at higher elevations, so appropriate gear and caution are advised.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on these bus-accessible trails?
A: The terrain in National Park Los Collados del Asón is primarily shaped by its karstic and glacial geology. You can expect a mix of limestone paths, rocky sections, and forest trails. Some routes may involve steep ascents and descents, particularly on the more difficult trails leading to peaks like Pico Porracolina. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended for most routes.
tips: One of the first peaks with beautiful views if you are doing the Lunada to Castro Valnera trail. Here you will have the first panoramic views, go carefully and firmly because the path is narrow.
Hill located between the Pico de la Miel and Castro Valnera, with great views of the two Peaks.
Modest peak near Alto de La Piluca. The views as great as in the rest of the mountain range to the Castro Valnera.
Castro Valnera is a beautiful, formidable mountain with its 1,718 meters. It is the highest in the eastern sector of the Cantabrian mountain range.
